headline: Two new model releases, NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 and Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, anchor a window dominated by architecture and pricing shifts.
body: NVIDIA shipped the Nemotron 3 series (Nano, Super, Ultra) pairing a hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and MTP, while Claude Fable 5 surfaced across reviews spanning computer vision, 3D reconstruction, and medical image classification. Transformer architecture and RLHF both register as breakouts, with post-training entering as a new concept. Pricing is the other axis of pressure: OpenAI is weighing price cuts against Anthropic, and a user reported Fable burning 45 percent of a weekly quota in a single day. Anthropic separately reversed a policy that would have silently limited Claude on frontier LLM development, drawing public criticism over transparency.

cited_items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ - CNBC
